title: Canthium telidosma
intro: Canthium telidosma, also known as the Telidosma tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its potential therapeutic effects in treating wounds, infections, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Canthium telidosma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Canthium telidosma is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, pain, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Canthium telidosma is classified under the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family.
